claim: Three Tier-1 experiments against Breuer 2019 labels on the 455-gene set all FAIL to beat the v15 keyword-prior detector baseline (MCC 0.5372). (1) Full-feature XGBoost stack (ESM-2 650M + ESMFold v1 + MACE-OFF + v15 outputs, 1305 cols): pooled 5-fold MCC 0.443, delta -0.094 vs v15. (2) Partition XGBoost + PCA-32 on the 165 v15-negative genes (96 current FNs, 69 current TNs), best global MCC = 0.530 at threshold 0.95 (delta -0.007, basically noise — only 1 call flipped). (3) kNN in L2-normalized ESM-2 space on the same partition, k in {5,10,20,30,50}: best partition-MCC 0.054 at k=50, global MCC 0.179 (delta -0.358). Conclusion: ESM-2 650M embeddings + ESMFold structure + MACE-OFF on 455 Syn3A CDS do NOT contain signal that XGBoost or kNN can extract to discriminate Breuer-essential from Breuer-nonessential beyond what v15's annotation-keyword priors already capture. The 1280:455 feature-to-row ratio is prohibitive, and within the lower-dim partition (165 rows, PCA-32) the embeddings still do not cluster essential-vs-nonessential. Tier-1 cache as populated is NOT a viable path to the 0.59 MCC target via direct supervised learning on Breuer labels alone.
source_detail: Reproducible via 'python scripts/train_tier1_xgboost.py --save-oof'. Inputs: the four cache parquets at commit 2728512 + outputs/predictions_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_composed_all455_v15_round2_priors.csv. Output at outputs/tier1_xgboost.json + outputs/predictions_tier1_xgboost_union.csv. Config: XGBClassifier(n_estimators=400, max_depth=4, learning_rate=0.05, subsample=0.8, colsample_bytree=0.5, min_child_weight=5, reg_lambda=1.0), scale_pos_weight set per-fold, StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42).

partition_xgboost_scan.note: No threshold beats v15; closest is thr=0.95 at delta -0.0072

partition_knn_esm2.note: Pure ESM-2 cosine kNN is catastrophic; embeddings do not cluster essentiality

caveats: Negative result. The naive 'concat all features + XGBoost' approach is falsified at MCC 0.44 vs v15's 0.54 — do not re-run this experiment without changing the design. Specifically: restricting XGBoost to the 96 FN / 72 TN subset (where v15 is silent) is the natural follow-up and is NOT ruled out by this fact.

Dataset is severely imbalanced (pos_rate=0.842, 383 positive vs 72 negative). v15's almost-zero FP rate (3/290 = 1%) is very hard to beat because any added-feature-induced FP costs more MCC than a recovered TP gains.

esmfold_disorder_fraction was dropped from the feature set: the 0.1.0-scale esmfold_v1.parquet has plddt 0-1 with a disorder threshold of 50, so every row's disorder_fraction is 1.0 (constant feature, no information). After the 0.2.0 unit-scale fix regenerates the parquet, a re-run would include it — unlikely to change the headline result since it's a single feature among 1295.

MACE-OFF covers only 111/455 genes with real kcat estimates; the remaining 344 are NaN-padded. XGBoost's native NaN handling means these are routed via default branch in each split, so the effective information from MACE is limited to the metabolic-enzyme subset.

Future direction that is ruled IN: partition-based modeling — train Tier-1 XGBoost only on the (FN, TN) subset where v15's binary call is 'nonessential', and apply it as a refinement only on v15-negative predictions. This isolates the classifier from the near-perfect v15-positive regime. 168 rows is tiny but the class balance is better (96 pos vs 72 neg).
No-information baseline (always predict positive) would give MCC = 0 (undefined: no negatives predicted) on this dataset. XGBoost at 0.145 on Tier-1 alone is better than random but nowhere near v15.
